Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add tex"..." LaTeX string macro for textbook-style tensor expressions

Recursive descent parser translates LaTeX tensor notation into TensorExpr:
  tex"R_{abcd}"                    → Tensor with down indices
  tex"g^{ab} R_{abcd}"            → product via juxtaposition
  tex"R_{ab} - \frac{1}{2} g_{ab} R" → sums with rational coefficients
  tex"\partial_a V^b"             → TDeriv
  tex"R^a{}_{bcd}"                → mixed indices with {} separator
  tex"\Gamma^a_{bc}"              → LaTeX Greek command support
  tex"F^A_{\mu\nu}"               → Unicode Greek index names

Features:
- Full LaTeX Greek letter mapping (\alpha → α, \Gamma → Γ, etc.)
- \frac{N}{M} and N/M rational coefficients
- \partial and \nabla derivative operators (also Unicode ∂, ∇)
- Nested derivatives: \partial_a \partial_b T_c
- Parenthesized subexpressions
- \cdot and \times as explicit multiplication (optional)
- parse_tex() runtime function + @tex_str compile-time macro

78 new tests (2,631 total, all passing).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix W5/W7/W8: year fallback, CPC keyword mapping, prospect selection

W5 (startability_ranking): Smarter year auto-fallback. When year=2024
returns sparse data (1 cluster), falls back to year with most data
(2023 with 607 clusters). Uses _best_year_ss() helper.

W7 (invention_intelligence): Added _KEYWORD_CPC mapping and
_cpc_keyword_fallback() function. Maps Japanese/English tech terms
directly to CPC codes (e.g., '全固体電池' → H01M). Fixes cluster
matching failure for battery-related queries.

W8 (sales_prospect): Fixed _get_scores() to use year with most data
instead of MAX(year). Fixed prospect selection to require gate_open=1,
filtering out zero-tech firms (bookstores) from licensing targets.

commit.message:
Balance: map difficulty 15% scaling, weapon buffs, new Health Regen passive, expanded meta progression

- Maps now scale exactly 15% harder each step (Fields→Crypt→Abyss): normalize spawn intervals, count_base, elite_chance
- Magic Bolt: +56% damage (28.08), +44% cast rate (0.504s cooldown)
- Boomerang: +21% damage, +17% orbit speed, added knockback (150→200 force)
- New passive: Vital Stone (health regen 1.5 HP/s per level), replaces Magnet
- GravitonRing evolution reassigned to Boomerang + Vital Stone
- Vampiric Fang restored to passive pool
- Meta shop: 5 tiers → 7 tiers, final tier cost +30% higher
- Fix weapon descriptions to match actual damage values across all base weapons

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: avatar errors, display name errors, privacy AppBar, template nav, version, email fn stub

Item 1 — Avatar upload: add catch block to _pickAndUploadAvatar so
upload/network failures show a SnackBar instead of silently failing.

Item 2 — Display name: wrap updateDisplayName call in try/catch so save
failures surface a SnackBar to the user.

Item 5 — Privacy settings screen: remove missed logo/title from AppBar
(was still showing SVG row + wrong title 'Notifications'); improve
_sendTestDigest error message with distinct copy for network errors,
undeployed function (404), and email-provider config issues; add
supabase/functions/send-weekly-digest/index.ts Deno stub with TODO
comments for wiring up a real email provider.

Item 10 — Template navigation: replace context.pop() + context.push()
in CreateDecisionScreen._submit() with context.go() so that after
creating a decision via the Templates settings screen the back button
leads to the main shell rather than back to Settings > Templates.

Item 17 — Version: add package_info_plus 9.0.0; create
packageInfoProvider (FutureProvider<PackageInfo>); replace hardcoded
'1.0.0' in settings_screen with '${info.version} (${info.buildNumber})'
read from the platform at runtime.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: split long text_run to avoid Feishu 400 errors (#26)

* Add recurring cron-based scheduled tasks

Support recurring tasks using standard 5-field cron expressions
(e.g. "0 8 * * 1-5" for weekdays at 8am). Features include:

- Cron expression validation and next-occurrence calculation
- Timezone support (default: Asia/Shanghai, configurable via SCHEDULE_TIMEZONE)
- Pause/resume/cancel lifecycle for recurring tasks
- Backward-compatible persistence (old format auto-migrated)
- setTimeout overflow handling for delays > 24.8 days
- API: POST cronExpr to /api/schedule, pause/resume endpoints
- CLI: mb schedule cron/pause/resume commands
- 22 new tests (cron-utils + recurring task lifecycle)
- Add "Feature Completion Workflow" rule to CLAUDE.md

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add MetaMemory → Feishu Wiki sync feature

Implements one-way sync from MetaMemory documents to a Feishu Wiki space.
The folder tree maps to wiki nodes; each document becomes a Feishu docx page
with Markdown-to-blocks conversion. Content hash comparison enables incremental sync.

New modules:
- src/sync/doc-sync.ts — Core sync service (syncAll, syncDocument)
- src/sync/sync-store.ts — SQLite persistence for sync mappings
- src/sync/markdown-to-blocks.ts — Markdown → Feishu block converter

Integration:
- /sync and /sync status chat commands
- POST/GET /api/sync and POST /api/sync/document API endpoints
- Auto-wired into Feishu bot bridges at startup
- Environment vars: WIKI_SYNC_ENABLED, WIKI_SPACE_NAME, WIKI_SYNC_THROTTLE_MS

Tests: 58 new tests across 3 test files (sync-store, markdown-to-blocks, doc-sync)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix wiki sync: table rendering and WIKI_SPACE_ID support

- Convert Markdown tables to code blocks instead of Feishu table blocks
  (Feishu documentBlockChildren API doesn't support inline table cell creation)
- Add WIKI_SPACE_ID env var for pre-configured wiki space
- Improve ensureWikiSpace() to use first available space when name doesn't match
- Better error messages for wiki space permission issues

Tested: full sync of 18 docs + 9 folders to Feishu Wiki space successfully

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix wiki sync: relative links, quote blocks, and double-traversal

- Strip relative URLs in links (Feishu API requires absolute http/https URLs)
- Convert blockquotes to plain text blocks (Feishu API doesn't support bare quote blocks)
- URL-encode link URLs with non-ASCII characters
- Fix double-traversal: filter root-level doc listing to skip subfolder docs
- Add folder_id to DocumentSummary interface
- Remove unused parseTable function
- Add tests for URL encoding and relative link handling

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Clean up wiki debug scripts and add plan-mode auto-respond

- Remove all wiki debug/test scripts from scripts/ (development-only utilities)
- Auto-respond to ExitPlanMode/EnterPlanMode interactive tools in the SDK
  stream, preventing the agent from hanging when it enters plan mode
- Add drainAutoRespondTools() to StreamProcessor for detecting and
  handling interactive tool pauses

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Update README: test count to 155, add wiki sync env vars

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Remove Agent Communication curl examples from README

Agent communication is now handled via the /metabot skill shortcut,
no need to document raw curl commands.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add Feishu doc reader, auto wiki sync, plan display, and MetaSkill improvements

Four major upgrades:

1. Feishu Document Reader: New `feishu-doc` skill and `fd` CLI to read
   Feishu/Lark documents as Markdown. API endpoint at GET /api/feishu/document.
   Includes block-to-markdown converter (reverse of markdown-to-blocks).

2. Auto Wiki Sync: MetaMemory changes now auto-sync to Feishu Wiki with
   5-second debounce. Incremental sync for 1-10 docs, full sync fallback
   for bulk changes. New EventEmitter-based memory-events module.

3. Plan Mode Display: When Claude exits plan mode, the plan file content
   is automatically sent to the Feishu user as a separate card message.
   StreamProcessor tracks plan file paths from Write tool calls.

4. MetaSkill Improvements: Removed redundant tech-lead sub-agent (CLAUDE.md
   is the orchestrator). Added MetaMemory integration for cross-session
   shared knowledge in generated agent teams.

Bug fixes:
- Auto-detect Claude binary path via `which claude` instead of hardcoded path
- Fix bot registry name collision when Feishu and Telegram bots share names
  (use platform:name keys, add getByPlatform/listByPlatform methods)
- Add Telegram video/audio/voice/animation message handlers

Also updates: install.sh (feishu-doc skill + fd CLI), .env.example (all
current config options), README.md, README_zh.md, and CLAUDE.md.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Add install.ps1: full-featured PowerShell installer matching install.sh
  (phases 0-9: prerequisites, clone, deps, interactive config, skills, PM2)
- Update ecosystem.config.cjs: use path.join() for cross-platform log paths
- Update README.md and README_zh.md with Windows install instructions
- executor.ts cross-platform changes were already on dev branch

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Extract useStoreBootstrap composable, eliminating repeated store init boilerplate

All four workspace-synced stores (workspace, user, lastMessageSeen, shoulderTap)
shared the same watch([isConnected, socketWorkspaceSlug], ...) pattern with
workspace-change detection, since-cursor reset, and updateSince logic.

useStoreBootstrap({ workspaceSlug, since, extraSources, onReset, onConnect })
owns that scaffolding. Stores declare their own state refs, pass them in, and
provide domain-specific onReset/onConnect callbacks.

Port all game improvements to the live server (public/)

Performance:

- Enemy movement and collision now use stored coords — no layout thrashing

Gameplay fixes:
- Fix helper/decoy: store helperPos as {x,y} so attracted enemies actually chase it
- Decoy now glows (box-shadow) to be visually distinct

Touch controls:
- Replace 8-directional swipe detection with follow-finger movement
- Player smoothly moves toward wherever the finger is

Game-over screen — vivid layered stats:
- Animated time counter counts up from 0 to final score
- Star rating (1-5) with staggered pop-in animation
- Rank label: JUST STARTED / KEEP GOING / NOT BAD / IMPRESSIVE / LEGENDARY (>=21s)
- 3x2 stats grid: time, best ever, steps, enemies dodged, closest call, proximity label
- Milestone badges for 10s/20s/30s if actually reached
- Score saved confirmation for logged-in users
- Leaderboard button integrated into panel

UX:
- Score format: "5.04s" instead of "5s042"

- Milestone flash at 10s/20s/30s speed-up events
- Restart prompt appears after 2.5s with blinking animation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix coordinate scaling: send full-resolution screenshots to planner

Screenshots were resized to 1280px before sending to the model, but
mouse coordinates were in original screen space (2560x1440). The model
gave coordinates on the resized image, causing misclicks.

Fix: send screenshots at full resolution as JPEG (quality 75 for size)
so model coordinates map 1:1 to screen coordinates.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: create ~/.openclaw before npm install in Dockerfile

Omniclaw's postinstall script runs `ln -sfn "$PWD" ~/.openclaw/current-plugin`
which fails during docker build because /home/node/.openclaw/ doesn't exist yet
(it's only created at runtime via volume mount). Pre-creating the directory
lets the symlink succeed during build.

commit.message:
Add Texture Editor for viewing, replacing, and creating game texture IGBs

New PySide6 GUI window for editing any game texture (loading screens, HUD,
fonts, skins, models). Builds brand-new PS2 CLUT IGBs from scratch so output
works in both XML2 and MUA regardless of source format.

Features:
- Open IGB: load textures from any existing IGB (DXT3/DXT5/CLUT)
- Create from Image: build new IGBs from PNG/TGA/BMP/JPG files
- Replace individual textures with auto-scale and auto-flip
- Export resolution selector (Original/2048/1024/512)
- Swap R/B channels, Show Mipmaps toggle
- Optimized CLUT quantization with vectorized numpy nearest-color

Bumps version to 0.3.0.
